question: Which of the following data elements are key to an effective Software Asset Management practice within ServiceNow? (Choose four.)

explanation: Discovery is best for large environments, Import data for medium batches, and Manual for small, ad-hoc entries.

question: Within Software Asset Management there are many key terms to understand, what is the best definition for Normalization?

options.A: One or more use rights assigned to a specific device or user
options.C: The process of standardizing discovered software installation to defined norms
options.D: The process of producing a compliance position by comparing the number of software rights acquired against the normalized software inventory
options.E: Software license details that define use rights

question: Which discovery sources are recommended by ServiceNow to populate ServiceNow software installation table? (Choose two.)

Client access is the right to use for a specific amount of time.
Coalesce can only use a single field to uniquely identify a record.
When receiving an order, which data point is mandatory?

options.B: They are activated by the sam_developer user
options.C: They are activated by the sam_admin user
options.D: They are requested from and activated by ServiceNow support
options.E: They are activated by default in the base ServiceNow system

question: Which of the following are true of plugins? (Choose two.)

options.A: Plugins can be disabled or deactivated when no longer in use.
options.B: Plugins can be activated by default in the base ServiceNow system.
options.C: When a plugin is active you cannot hide its functionality.
options.D: Plugins are stand-alone and do not depend on other plugins being activated.
options.E: Plugins are software components that provide specific features and functionalities.

options.C: The first stage in the CSD process flow is configuration.

options.B: The software discovery model has not yet completed its run through the normalization process.
options.C: A discovery model was normalized based on publisher and product fields only.

question: What is the name of the Scheduled job that runs daily for normalization?

options.A: One or more use rights assigned to a specific device or user.
options.C: The process of normalizing a discovered software installation to standardized values.
options.D: Finding and recognizing software or a software feature on a device.

question: How often is the scheduled job for the normalization process run?

options.D: Nightly for new discovery models and weekly for all discovery models that do not have a status of normalized or manually normalized
options.E: Immediately for new discovery models and nightly for all discovery models that do not have a status of normalized or manually normalized

question: Discovery models may not be fully normalized until updated content is downloaded from the ServiceNow Software Content Library. How do you determine if the content has been downloaded?

options.A: Review the Last updated date of the Central Data Service Download Status jobs on the Normalization and Content Service dashboard.
options.B: Determine if the business rule “Create a Software Normalization Download” has been triggered yet or not.
options.C: Query the scheduled job reports to determine if the “Software Content Library Download” (samp_cds_download) job has completed yet.
options.D: Review the number of records in the samp_product_map table. If there are ~7,700 records in the table, the download has completed at least once.

options.A: Are you there? How will I classify you?

options.B: Run the “Migrate software installations” script to copy records from the [cmdb_ci_spkg] table to the [cmdb_sam_sw_install] table
options.C: Run the “Discover software installations” script to copy records from the [cmdb_ci_spkg] table to the [cmdb_sam_sw_install] table

options.B: They are automatically created during discovery

options.B: To track, control, and reduce software costs, including costs associated with non-compliance findings during a software audit

question: The Per Named Device license metric licenses which of the following?

options.A: A user for a number of installations of software

options.D: Cores on a physical server or virtual machine
options.E: A specific device for a number of installations of software

options.C: To select a category of specific license entitlement attributes

question: ServiceNow Performance Analytics identifies a trend that use of a certain software package has reduced over time. This is a potential indicator of what?

options.B: Your company needs to renegotiate your contract to get better pricing.
options.C: Your company is over-licensed for the product.
options.D: Your company needs to retire the unused entitlements.

options.B: Know what you have so you can manage it.
options.C: Improve efficiency and effectiveness through integration with other areas, such as request, procurement, and IT Service Management.

question: Match the reconciliation result description with the correct ServiceNow SAM result type.
explanation: In ServiceNow SAM, a Product Result is the highest level, generated for any licensable product found. A Software Model Result is only created if an entitlement or specific software model is defined. The License Metric Result is the most granular, directly comparing rights owned versus rights used to determine compliance and remediation options.

question: Blacklisted candidates are ______________ .

options.A: Software installations that are not normalized
options.C: Software installations not allowed in the network environment

question: Which of the following is not a core Software Asset Management function?

options.A: Workflow(s) or process(es) have been initiated to allocate software rights
options.B: Rights are now available and can be consumed during reconciliation
options.C: Workflow(s) or process(es) have been initiated to purchase software rights
options.D: Workflow(s) or process(es) have been initiated to harvest software rights
options.E: Customer instance is pending updates to CDS Server

question: Which stage of the software asset lifecycle includes all of the following steps? Discovering software in the environment, Normalizing installed software to standards, Reconciling entitlements to discovered software, Remediating detected variances

A software model can have up to how many catalog items?
Match each catalog type with its correct definition.

pairs.Product Catalog: Collection of models in the environment.
pairs.Service Catalog: Listing of goods an organization provides.
pairs.Vendor Catalog: Listing of goods offered or available by a third party.

explanation: Product Catalog: models in the environment. Service Catalog: goods the organization provides internally. Vendor Catalog: goods available from a third party.

question: If a user requests three items from the software catalog at the same time, how many individual requests are created?

What is the purpose of an external audit?

options.A: To ensure your users are not running any blacklisted software
options.B: To determine compliance with internally-defined vendor processes
options.C: To determine compliance with signed vendor agreements/contracts
options.D: To ensure your entitlements do not exceed your allocations
options.E: To determine compliance with licensing agreements regarding who, how, and when freeware software is being used

question: Match each software licensing term with its correct definition.

pairs.Downgrade/Upgrade: Software entitlement rights to utilize previous or future versions.
pairs.Software Subscription: Ability to utilize the software for a limited amount of time.
pairs.Client Access: Specific number of users or devices that can access a product.

explanation: Downgrade/Upgrade: contractual right to switch to a previous or future version. Software Subscription: time-limited license that must be renewed periodically. Client Access: defines the number of authorized users or devices (CAL). Publisher Pack: SAM bundle providing compliance dashboards and reporting tools from the publisher.

question: What are the available actions associated with the Normalization Suggestions feature? (Choose two.)

options.A: Accept – updates the discovery model with suggested values
options.B: Reject – ignores the suggestion. The next time the scheduled job is executed, it will not pick the same package or rule that was rejected previously
options.C: Save – allows you to save the suggestion to review again later
options.D: Ignore – ignores the suggestion. All fields are updated to read only until the next time the job is executed with the same package or rule that was ignored previously.
options.E: Create – creates a discovery model with suggested values
